title: dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. russowii
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. russowii, also known as Russow's marsh orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the Dactylorhiza genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. russowii are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is highly restricted due to its endangered status and limited availability.
